channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Indian Embassy in Iran Advises Nationals to Stay Indoors Amid Escalating US-Israel Attacks
item.description: The Indian Embassy in Iran has issued a precautionary advisory for Indian nationals currently in Iran, urging them to stay indoors and avoid military installations or multi-storey buildings for the ne...

item.description: In the midst of escalating U.S. military action against Iran and rising concern about a broader regional conflict, House Democratic leaders are calling for lawmakers to return to Washington immediatel...

item.title: The Hidden Environmental Impact of Vegan Leather
item.description: While many brands market 'vegan leather' as an eco-friendly and animal-friendly alternative to traditional leather, the reality is more complicated. Vegan leather can be made from various materials, i...

item.title: Canavan urges comprehensive economic reform and protective measures for Australian industries
item.description: Nationals leader Matt Canavan has called for a fundamental reshaping of Australia's economic approach, advocating for permanent tariffs and stronger support for domestic manufacturing. Speaking ahead ...

item.title: Authorities Arrest Nine for Illegal Sale of Petroleum Worth P9.7M in Navotas
item.description: Nine individuals were apprehended in Navotas City for their involvement in the illegal sale and distribution of petroleum products valued at approximately P9.7 million. The operation, conducted by the...

item.title: Speedy Gonzales Animated Feature Film in Development
item.description: After decades of controversy surrounding cultural stereotypes, Speedy Gonzales is poised for a revival on the big screen. Warner Bros. is developing an animated feature film directed by Jorge R. Gutié...

item.title: Barangay Health Workers Rally for Magna Carta on World Health Day
item.description: On April 7, 2026, coinciding with World Health Day, barangay health workers (BHWs) from various federations across the Philippines gathered at the University of the Philippines Diliman to demand the p...

item.title: Kanye West denied entry to the UK over controversial views
item.description: The Wireless music festival in the UK was cancelled after rapper Kanye West, also known as Ye, was blocked from entering the country. The British government’s interior minister cited that his presence...

item.title: Armed attack targets Israeli Consulate building in Istanbul
item.description: Early on April 7, 2026, gunmen launched an attack on the building housing the Israeli Consulate in Istanbul, Turkey. The incident has heightened security concerns in the city, particularly around dipl...

item.title: Monthly Collection of David Rowe's Political Cartoons
item.description: This article provides an overview of the latest political cartoons by David Rowe for April 2026. Renowned for his sharp wit and satirical take on federal politics, Rowe offers a visual commentary on c...

item.title: South Africa Advocates for Equitable Sharing of Biodiversity Benefits
item.description: Deputy Minister of Forestry, Fisheries and the Environment, Narend Singh, has urged the international community to ensure fairness in the distribution of benefits arising from biodiversity. Speaking a...

item.title: Pakistan’s international influence claims questioned by UAE and Saudi Arabia
item.description: The article discusses Pakistan's recent attempts to project itself as a global power, analysing the reactions from key Gulf states like the UAE and Saudi Arabia. Despite Pakistan's efforts to present ...

item.title: Joyous Celebration Directors Dispute Financial Management as Group Marks 30 Years
item.description: As Joyous Celebration marks three decades in the South African gospel scene, tensions between its directors have come to light. Co-director Lindelani Mkhize has accused fellow director Jabu Hlongwane ...

item.title: Deutsche Bank Explains Why Rising Oil Prices Haven’t Weakened Global Equities
item.description: Despite a strong rise in oil prices pushing Brent crude above $100 per barrel, global stock markets have shown unexpected stability, according to an analysis by Deutsche Bank. The bank’s strategists s...

item.title: A parent’s exploration of what makes a truly good children’s restaurant menu
item.description: In this Guardian feature, a parent undertakes a month-long quest across the UK to discover what makes a genuinely good children’s restaurant menu. The article opens with the familiar picture of predic...

item.title: Tiny Takeover Challenge Concludes with Reward Claiming
item.description: The Tiny Takeover event has come to a close, with the final challenge officially completed. The 'land babies' team triumphed, successfully keeping many baby mobs wild and energetic, while the 'water b...

item.title: Mokoka sets sights on extending his Two Oceans Half-Marathon legacy as Seloyi defends his title
item.description: Stephen Mokoka, one of the most successful runners in Two Oceans Half-Marathon history, is determined to add a fifth podium finish to his impressive career. Mokoka, a four-time winner of the event, is...

item.title: Max Chandler-Mather returns as head of Greens Institute to advance progressive policies
item.description: Max Chandler-Mather has resumed leadership of the Greens Institute, the policy think-tank of the Australian Greens, with the goal of energising left-wing politics and building a mass movement capable ...

item.title: Addressing the Issue of Skilled Migrants Driving Taxis Despite Their Qualifications
item.description: The article highlights the difficulties skilled migrants face when their qualifications from overseas are not recognised in Australia, leading them to work in low-skill jobs like taxi driving. Former ...

item.title: Exploring Family, Power, and Girlhood in Madeline Cash’s Debut Novel
item.description: Madeline Cash’s debut novel, 'Lost Lambs', paints a vivid portrait of a suburban family grappling with contemporary anxieties. The Flynns, a seemingly ordinary family, face unconventional challenges: ...
